title: Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - LXIII
content:
I Hoed and trenched and weeded,
    And took the flowers to fair:
    I brought them home unheeded;
    The hue was not the wear.

    So up and down I sow them
    For lads like me to find,
    When I shall lie below them,
    A dead man out of mind.

    Some seed the birds devour,
    And some the season mars,
    But here and there will flower
    The solitary stars,

    And fields will yearly bear them
    As light-leaved spring comes on,
    And luckless lads will wear them
    When I am dead and gone.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alfred Edward Housman delivers a powerful performance in "Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - LXIII"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
